en: What an icebreaker. It's an icebreaker. Now we all get to argue whether this is even a good question to ask.
I think we all know it's not. Oh!
ko: 다들 그게 아니라는 걸 알겠죠. 오!

en: Okay, all right. We'll move on. Uh, in my honest opinion, providing wheelchair accessibility is not
something to focus on. I think that's something that you should focus on.
Good. Thank you for not letting him get away with wheelchair accessibility doesn't matter.
ko: 좋아. 그가 휠체어 접근성이 중요하지 않다고 하지 않게 해줘서 고마워.

en: Well, there's laws in place, right? The state, local laws should have already focused on that.
That's already taken care of. I took it to mean that you wouldn't even throw an event at a place

ko: 나는 네가 휠체어 접근성이 없는 곳에서는 이벤트를 열지 않을 거라고 이해했어. 응. 알겠어.
오, 그들은 정당하네. 전문성을 보여줘. 자, 여러분, 내가 겪었던 실제 경험이 있어. 2009년이야.

en: Be a beacon of professionalism. So, guys, here's a real-life experience I had to deal with.

ko: 나는 피스카타웨이에 있었어. 은퇴 파티를 조직했지.

en: Organize a retirement party. So the guy wife pulls me aside and makes it very clear she has an attraction to me.
ko: 그래서 그 남자의 아내가 나를 옆으로 불러서 나에게 끌린다고 분명히 말했어.

en: What do I do? I resist the urge, I stay professional, and I DM her after the event.
ko: 나는 어떻게 해야 할까? 유혹을 참아.
